checks.finding: design.md ## Architecture (lines 16-39) provides a mermaid flowchart with named subgraphs `upstream` (new, opt-in) and `downstream` (existing plugins), showing component boundaries (DM, DI, DR, DRL, CMV, HA, DS) and integration into existing components (DS -> B[sdd-bootstrap Phase 1] -> RL[spec/impl review loops] -> QG[quality-gate] -> WR[workflow-retrospective], with a WFI/diagnose feedback edge). Technology choices per layer are given in the adjoining ## Components table (SKILL.md, agent defs, PowerShell+bash, JSON Schema) and ## Layer Specifications table (UX/Frontend N-A/Infra/Security). The narrative sentence 'Flow stays unidirectional... feedback returns only via WFI/diagnose' describes interaction behavior, not just naming. All three required elements (boundaries, integration, technology per layer) are present with concrete detail, not a one-sentence description.

Dependency chain in ## Architecture is DM -> DI -> DRL -> CMV -> HA -> DS -> B -> RL -> QG -> WR, with DR feeding into DI on reverse mode. The only back-edge is WR -.-> DM, explicitly labeled 'drift / diagnose findings' and explained in the surrounding text as feedback that 'returns only via WFI/diagnose into a new domain-model update run' (design.md line 38-39) -- a distinct, human-mediated new invocation, not a direct runtime dependency cycle. No component both depends on and is depended upon by another within a single pipeline run. No cycle found.

## Data Plan (design.md lines 89-105) specifies Data Entities (bounded contexts, terms, aggregates, context relations, domain events, commands, policies, message flows), Existing Data Affected ('none (new domain/ directory; additive Bounded-Context: field in future requirements.md files)'), and Migration Strategy ('none required. Projects adopt by running /sdd-domain:domain-model; absence keeps legacy behavior (AC-010)'). All three required sub-fields are present and explicit.

## API / Contract Plan (design.md lines 107-117) lists the new contract schema (contracts/domain-contract.v1.schema.json with its envelope and relation enum), states verdict output reuse (review-contract.v1 stage:domain, cross-model-verdict/v1 unchanged), and explicitly states 'No HTTP APIs.' This is adequate coverage for a CLI/skill-orchestration feature with no HTTP endpoints; no deprecated/breaking existing endpoints are claimed or contradicted elsewhere in the spec set.

## Security Boundaries (design.md lines 135-144) names four trust boundaries (seed inputs -> interviewer, agent -> approval lines, domain-contract.json -> gates, and the previously-missing row 'Sanitized domain-artifact bundle -> external vendor models (cross-model-verify)'). The corrected row states the auth mechanism ('Existing vendor API auth (reused unchanged); input-manifest allowlist limits the bundle to domain/*'), data classification ('internal -- must exclude real person names and customer-identifying values (role/system names only)'), and OWASP concern ('Information Disclosure'). This row is corroborated by security-spec.md B4 boundary (Trust Boundaries table and STRIDE row 'Confidential data in vendor bundle'), and by infra-spec.md's Data Residency note pointing to security-spec.md for exclusion rules. All four required items (trust boundaries, auth/authz per boundary, PII/data classification, OWASP concerns) are present across all four rows. The specific fix requested for this round -- the external-vendor-egress boundary row -- is present and complete.

SKIP: Feature Type header states 'framework plugin (new upstream lane in an existing multi-plugin repository)', which is not `fullstack`. Independently, frontend-spec.md and ux-spec.md both state 'N/A -- no change: no graphical frontend / conversational CLI only', so no frontend surface exists to check for consistency against the API/Contract Plan. Per calibration Cannot-Verify Handling, SKIP applies because the scoped surface (a frontend layer) does not exist.

## Test Strategy (design.md lines 119-133) covers Unit scope (Pester: schema validation fixtures valid/corrupt, conformance check fixtures conformant/deviant plus two-context fixtures for AC-015, guard line-diff tests, template language checks, validate-repository expectations), Integration scope (end-to-end artifact-set generation, domain-sync injection fixture, absence regression, update-mode re-run semantics for AC-016, cross-model gate fixtures for AC-006/AC-017), and acceptance-criteria-to-test mapping is concretely named per AC (e.g. AC-015, AC-016, AC-006, AC-017 cited directly in-line). Concrete planned evidence is named: specific fixture file paths (tests/sdd-domain/*.Tests.ps1, tests/hooks/domain-approval-guard.Tests.ps1) consistent with acceptance-tests.md TEST-001..TEST-017. This is a CLI/skill-orchestration feature with no graphical user journey, so no separate E2E/user-journey artifact is required beyond the named integration fixtures; the Windows-local constraint (PS 5.1 + Git Bash, no non-ASCII in .ps1) is also stated as planned execution context.

All component labels in the ## Architecture diagram (DM=domain-model, DI=domain-interviewer, DR=domain-reverse, DRL=domain-review-loop, CMV=cross-model-verify, HA=Human approval, DS=domain-sync) are defined in the ## Components table (lines 43-56) by name and responsibility. Downstream diagram nodes B (sdd-bootstrap Phase 1), RL (spec/impl review loops), QG (quality-gate), and WR (workflow-retrospective) are established existing system components identifiable from requirements.md Assumptions ('sdd-forge v1.8.0 structures... are available for reuse', 'Cross-model panelist agents... from sdd-quality-loop are reusable') and from the design.md Layer Specifications/API Contract Plan text referencing these as pre-existing plugins, satisfying the established-system-component exception. No undefined component reference found.

design.md's ## ADR Change Log (lines 83-87) references ADR-0004 ('DDD upstream lane as seventh plugin with seven-stage pipeline, cross-model-verified domain gate, warn-first conformance', Status: Accepted). The file docs/adr/0004-ddd-upstream-domain-lane.md exists on disk (verified via directory listing, 2704 bytes). The feature's core architectural departure (new plugin lane, cross-model-verified gate) is covered by this ADR; no undocumented new architectural decision was found requiring an additional ADR.

design.md ## Design System Compliance (line 71) states 'N/A -- ds_profile: none', consistent with ux-spec.md ## Design Tokens ('N/A -- ds_profile: none (repository has no design-system/)') and frontend-spec.md's overall N/A frontend declaration. sdd-domain ships no graphical UI, so no design-token or component-library conformance surface exists. The N/A declaration is reasoned and consistent across all three specs, not merely omitted.
